proposed_mechanism: Rank contracts by trailing realized OKX funding-rate volatility and trade a weekly cross-sectional premium for bearing uncertain financing costs.
proposed_payer: Leveraged perpetual traders seeking stable financing costs and participants forced to reduce positions when funding becomes unstable.

data_feasibility.source: official OKX realized funding-rate archives already stored locally

duplicate_audit.H21_price_funding_disagreement: same funding cross-section and crowded-position payer; H21 was already rejected before PnL for boundary ties and momentum-family overlap
duplicate_audit.I017_lagged_funding_persistence_carry: same realized funding archive and adjacent-settlement crowding channel; rejected for coverage, turnover, reversal overlap and insufficient carry-to-cost scale
duplicate_audit.I018_time_series_funding_sign_carry: same leveraged funding payer; direct funding cashflow failed across every symbol after costs

direction_audit.long_high_uncertainty_interpretation: risk compensation for bearing unstable financing costs
direction_audit.short_high_uncertainty_interpretation: crowding and deleveraging pressure predicts underperformance
direction_audit.reason: Both directions are economically plausible and the available primary literature supports the funding-price anchoring mechanism, not a unique cross-sectional return sign for funding volatility. Selecting a sign from historical returns would be outcome-driven.

no_rescue: do not choose long-high or short-high funding volatility after viewing returns
do not residualize against funding level or price momentum to manufacture independence
do not select a better volatility window
do not reopen H21, I017 or I018 under a new name
do not open PnL for this candidate
